title: Chapter 9 Master Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of file I/O and error handling.

questions.question: What mode opens a file for reading?
questions.explanation: "r" is read mode, the default.

questions.question: What does "w" mode do to an existing file?
questions.explanation: "w" erases the file before writing.

questions.question: Which mode appends to a file?
questions.explanation: "a" adds content to the end of the file.

questions.question: You should always use the 'with' statement for file operations.
questions.explanation: 'with' auto-closes files and prevents resource leaks.

questions.question: What does readlines() return?
questions.explanation: readlines() returns all lines as a list.

questions.question: To write Python data to JSON, use json._____().
questions.explanation: json.dump() writes Python data to a file in JSON format.

questions.question: What module reads JSON files?
questions.explanation: The built-in 'json' module handles JSON.

questions.question: What does the except block do?
questions.explanation: except catches and handles specific errors from the try block.

questions.question: When does finally execute?
questions.explanation: finally ALWAYS executes, error or not.

questions.question: json.load() reads JSON from a file.
questions.explanation: json.load() reads and parses a JSON file into Python data.

questions.question: What error occurs when accessing a missing file?
questions.explanation: FileNotFoundError is raised when the file doesn't exist.

questions.question: The _____ block runs only if try succeeded (no errors).
questions.explanation: The else block in try/except runs only when try completes without errors.

questions.question: What does csv.reader() return?
questions.explanation: csv.reader() returns an iterator; each iteration gives a row as a list.

questions.question: The 'with' statement automatically calls close().
questions.explanation: 'with' auto-closes the resource when the block ends.

questions.question: What does strip() do when reading file lines?
questions.explanation: strip() removes leading/trailing whitespace including \n newlines.

questions.question: What error does int('abc') raise?
questions.explanation: ValueError is raised when a value can't be converted to the target type.

questions.question: CSV stands for Comma-Separated Values.
questions.explanation: CSV is a plain-text format using commas to separate fields.

questions.question: What happens if try has no matching except?
questions.explanation: If no except matches the error, the program crashes with a traceback.

questions.question: What does json.dumps() do (with 's')?
questions.explanation: json.dumps() converts Python data to a JSON string (not file).

questions.question: To read a JSON file: data = json._____(file).
questions.explanation: json.load() reads and parses JSON from a file object.

questions.question: What is the purpose of try/except?
questions.explanation: try/except prevents crashes by catching and handling errors.

questions.question: You can have multiple except blocks for different errors.
questions.explanation: You can chain multiple except blocks to handle different error types.
